title: Unrestricted File Upload
principle: Decide what a file IS by its content and your rules, not by its name. Validate the type by sniffing content, generate your own random storage name with a safe extension, store outside the web root (or on object storage) so it can't be executed, and cap the size. Never trust the client-supplied filename or Content-Type.

snippets.library: a content-type allowlist + a generated name outside web root
snippets.secure:
import os, secrets, filetype       # imghdr was removed in Python 3.13
f = request.files['file']
head = f.stream.read(261); f.stream.seek(0)
kind = filetype.guess(head)
if kind is None or kind.mime not in {'image/png', 'image/jpeg'}: abort(400)
ext = {'image/png': '.png', 'image/jpeg': '.jpg'}[kind.mime]
name = secrets.token_hex(16) + ext
f.save(os.path.join(UPLOAD_DIR, name))   # UPLOAD_DIR is not web-served
snippets.note: f.filename is attacker-controlled (shell.php, ../..). Sniff the content, mint your own name, and store where the server won't execute it.

snippets.library: multer with a size limit + server-side type check + random name
snippets.vulnerable: const upload = multer({ dest: 'public/uploads/' }); // keeps original name/type
snippets.secure:
const upload = multer({ storage: multer.memoryStorage(), limits: { fileSize: 5e6 } });
// then: verify magic bytes (file-type), write crypto.randomUUID()+ext to a non-served dir
snippets.note: Don't persist under a served path with the original name. Check the real type from magic bytes (the 'file-type' package), not req.file.mimetype.
getOriginalFilename() is untrusted. Detect the type from content, generate the name, and keep the directory off the servlet path.

references.title: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
